================================================================================
GitHub Actions — repository secrets for CI/CD
================================================================================

Add these in GitHub:

  Repository → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret

Use the exact names below (they must match what your workflow references).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RENDER_DEPLOY_HOOK_URL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
What it is:
  A unique HTTPS URL that triggers a new deploy of your Render web service when
  requested (e.g. POST from GitHub Actions after a push).

Where to find it:
  1. Log in to https://dashboard.render.com
  2. Open your backend web service (the Node service that runs server.js).
  3. Go to the "Settings" tab for that service.
  4. Scroll to "Deploy Hook" (or "Build & Deploy" → deploy hooks, depending on UI).
  5. Create a deploy hook if you do not have one; copy the full hook URL.

Value format:
  Typically: https://api.render.com/deploy/srv-xxxxxxxxxxxxx?key=yyyyyyyyyyyy

Security:
  Treat this like a password. Anyone with the URL can trigger deploys.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RENDER_BACKEND_URL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
What it is:
  The public base URL of your deployed backend API (no trailing slash is usual).

Where to find it:
  1. In the Render dashboard, open the same web service.
  2. At the top of the service page, use the onrender.com URL shown (e.g. under
     the service name), or check "Settings" for the custom domain if you added one.

Value format examples:
  https://your-service-name.onrender.com
  https://api.yourdomain.com   (if you use a custom domain)

Use in CI/CD for:
  Health checks, smoke tests, or any step that must call the live API
  (e.g. GET {RENDER_BACKEND_URL}/api/health).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VERCEL_TOKEN
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
What it is:
  A personal access token Vercel accepts for CLI/API operations (deployments,
  project linking, etc.), depending on how your workflow is written.

Where to find it:
  1. Log in to https://vercel.com
  2. Click your profile (avatar) → Account Settings (or team settings if the
     project lives under a team).
  3. Open "Tokens" (sometimes under "Security" or "Settings → Tokens").
  4. Create a new token with a descriptive name and an appropriate scope/expiry.
  5. Copy the token immediately; it is often shown only once.

Value format:
  A single opaque string (not a URL).

Security:
  Store only in GitHub Secrets, never in the repo or logs.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VERCEL_FRONTEND_URL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
What it is:
  The production URL where your static frontend is served after Vercel deploy.

Where to find it:
  1. In the Vercel dashboard, open the project that deploys this repo’s frontend.
  2. On the project "Overview" or "Deployments" tab, open the latest production
     deployment; the assigned domain is shown there.
  3. Alternatively: Project → Settings → Domains — use the primary production
     domain (e.g. *.vercel.app or your custom domain).

Value format examples:
  https://your-project.vercel.app
  https://www.yourdomain.com

Use in CI/CD for:
  E2E tests, link checks, or notifying PRs with the live preview/production URL.

================================================================================
Quick checklist
================================================================================
[ ] RENDER_DEPLOY_HOOK_URL   — Render → service → Settings → Deploy Hook
[ ] RENDER_BACKEND_URL       — Render → service → public URL (onrender.com or custom)
[ ] VERCEL_TOKEN             — Vercel → Account/Team → Tokens → create token
[ ] VERCEL_FRONTEND_URL      — Vercel → project → production domain

After saving secrets, re-run a failed workflow or push a small commit to verify
the pipeline can read them (secret values are never printed in logs if masked).
